title: Stalin's dacha — the night Barbarossa begins
datetime: 22 June 1941, the first hours of the German invasion
domain: WW2 command / information and denial under shock
known_outcome: The German invasion of the Soviet Union begins. Soviet leadership struggles through shock, delayed orders, and catastrophic early losses. The war becomes the Great Patriotic War; the initial hours remain a study in how power processes unbearable news.
decision_question: When invasion reports arrive that contradict preferred assumptions, does the center accept the reality of full-scale war quickly enough to issue coherent orders?

opening.cold_open: Before dawn on 22 June 1941, the largest invasion force in history crosses into the Soviet Union. At the political center, the first problem is not maps — it is belief. What kind of war is this, and who is allowed to say so?
opening.what_they_knew: There had been warnings. There had also been reasons, true and false, to distrust warnings. Once the frontier is burning, the question becomes tempo: how fast can a hyper-centralized system authorize the obvious?
opening.pressure: Personal risk of bringing bad news. Institutional habits of suspicion. The cost of delay measured in encirclements.

choices.label: Process through shock — delayed, centralized response
choices.summary: The historical path: confusion, delayed full recognition, and orders that cannot match the speed of the frontier collapse.
choices.immediate: Frontier districts absorb the first blow while the center catches up to the scale of the attack.
choices.near_term: Encircling battles and catastrophic losses mark the opening weeks.
choices.longer_arc: The USSR survives through later mobilization and depth — but the opening remains a permanent lesson in warning, belief, and command latency.
choices.image_prompt: 1941 Soviet dacha interior at night, maps and telephones, tense silhouettes, historical documentary painting, no text
choices.vo_script: Barbarossa did not begin as a clean briefing. It began as a fight between evidence and the stories powerful men preferred. The historical path is delay under shock — and a frontier that could not wait for the center to finish arguing with reality.

choices.label: Immediate full acceptance — war orders without theater
choices.summary: SPECULATION: The center treats the first credible reports as full-scale war and pushes coherent defensive authorization faster.
choices.immediate: Some units may receive clearer authority to fight as a country at war rather than a border incident.
choices.near_term: Even faster acceptance cannot erase German operational surprise — but it can change the shape of early chaos.
choices.longer_arc: This is simulated process improvement, not a guaranteed inverted war. Logistics, doctrine, and force posture still dominate. Label the optimism.
choices.image_prompt: night war room 1941, officers moving with urgency, telephones, maps of the western frontier, serious historical still, no text
choices.vo_script: What if the center believed the first true reports? This branch is speculation. Faster recognition is not a magic counteroffensive. It is a narrower question: how many hours of coherence can a shocked system buy when the frontier is already on fire?

choices.label: Double down on 'provocation' framing
choices.summary: SPECULATION: Leadership treats early reports as provocation or local excess longer than the historical record already shows.
choices.immediate: Orders remain confused; local commanders pay in encirclement.
choices.near_term: The opening disaster deepens. Political storytelling consumes operational time.
choices.longer_arc: A darker branch of an already dark opening. Simulated — and constrained by the fact that reality eventually becomes undeniable.
choices.image_prompt: dim office, unanswered phones, distant red horizon implying invasion, oppressive atmosphere, historical painting style, no text
choices.vo_script: There is a worse version of denial. Not the historical struggle to accept — but a longer performance that the fire is still a misunderstanding. We simulate it carefully. The frontier does not care about the performance.

sources: Established WW2 scholarship on 22 June 1941 and Soviet high-command response
Published documentary collections on the opening of the German-Soviet war

provenance.discipline: ELOSTIRION accuracy: decision-point framing; primary-source grounding; speculation labeled
provenance.notes: Public pack compresses a complex historiography into a playable decision. Counterfactual branches are labeled simulated.

style_lock: 1941 Soviet night interiors, heavy shadows, documentary gravity, no modern objects, no text
